title: casuarina collina
intro: Casuarina collina, also known as the Australian pine or she-oak, is a plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its conical shape and needle-like leaves. The medicinal properties of Casuarina collina include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and protect against infections, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Casuarina collina include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to make cough syrups, inhalants, and other medicinal products.
